name: Calañas, El Cerro de Andévalo – ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Calañas offer a diverse landscape for outdoor enthusiasts, situated in the heart of the Andévalo region of Huelva, Spain. The area features a mix of unpaved tracks, asphalt roads, and rolling plains and hills, providing varied terrain for mountain biking. The presence of the Odiel River and the Calabazar Reservoir adds scenic routes along their banks, while forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and pine trees offer shaded trails. This region's historical mining heritage also contributes unique backdrops for rides. Best mountain bike trails around Calañas The most popular mountain bike trail is Río Odiel, Embalse de Calabazar, Vía Verde del Camino – ruta circular, a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines river views, reservoir paths, and sections of a greenway. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Calañas, El Cerro de Andévalo – ruta circular, a moderate 21.7 miles (35.0 km) path. This route explores the rolling hills and diverse landscapes characteristic of the Andévalo region. Local mountain bikers also love the Bar FLC loop from El Gamonital, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Calañas is defined by rolling plains, river valleys, and Mediterranean forests, with routes that cater to different ability levels. The network includes options from moderate paths to more demanding trails. The routes in Calañas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calañas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Calañas?
A: Mountain biking in Calañas offers a diverse landscape, featuring a mix of unpaved tracks, asphalt roads, and rolling plains and hills. You'll find scenic routes along the Odiel River and the Calabazar Reservoir, as well as shaded trails through forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and pine trees. The region's historical mining heritage also provides unique backdrops for rides.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Calañas?
A: Yes, Calañas offers a variety of trails, including options suitable for beginners. Out of the 54 mountain bike routes available, 12 are classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Calañas has 11 difficult mountain bike routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge over the Odiel River – Odiel River loop from Valverde del Camino&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 65 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Calañas?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Calañas are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995750007/rio-odiel-embalse-de-calabazar-via-verde-del-camino-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Odiel, Embalse de Calabazar, Vía Verde del Camino – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995763960/calanas-el-cerro-de-andevalo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calañas, El Cerro de Andévalo – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Calañas pass by several interesting landmarks. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge over the Odiel River&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5346190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Molino de Acha&lt;/a&gt;, or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calabazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region's mining heritage also means you'll ride through unique landscapes shaped by its industrial past.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calañas?
A: The Huelva province, where Calañas is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Winter can also be suitable, with cooler weather making longer rides more comfortable.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Calañas?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 12 easy routes in Calañas are generally suitable for families with some biking experience. These trails typically have less challenging terrain and elevation, making them more accessible for a wider range of ages and skill levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Calañas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Calañas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views along the Odiel River and reservoir, and the unique historical context provided by the mining heritage.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: Calañas, as a town, offers various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. For specific options directly adjacent to trails, it's advisable to check local listings or plan your route to pass through the town center or other villages like Valverde del Camino, which also has amenities.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Calañas on average?
A: The mountain bike trails around Calañas vary significantly in length. While some routes are shorter, many are designed for longer excursions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar FLC loop from El Gamonital&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 45.6 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38189167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto – Vía Verde Molino de Acha loop from Fuente de la Corcha&lt;/a&gt; is around 52.4 km, offering substantial rides.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Calañas?
A: While Calañas is a rural area, local bus services connect it to larger towns in the Huelva province. However, direct public transport to specific trailheads might be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for connections to Calañas itself, and then consider cycling from the town center to your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to explore?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural monuments. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelamusa Water-Filled Quarry&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5733584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta de los Silos&lt;/a&gt;, both offering striking landscapes. The mountain of El Morante, barely a kilometer from Calañas, is also an area of geological interest.
tips:
Calañas, in the province of Huelva, is a town with a rich mining history, a privileged natural environment, and a deeply rooted culture.
Ancient Origins: It was a mining enclave from the Tartessian and Roman periods, with finds at "El Morante" including amphorae, tombstones, and coins.
Middle Ages: Under Muslim, then Christian, rule, it passed to the Guzmán family. Livestock farming was the main activity.
19th Century: Mining revived (pyrite, copper, iron, manganese), and the railway arrived in 1886. Calañas reached 12,000 inhabitants in 1910.
20th Century: The closure of mines and subsistence farming led to depopulation. Today, it seeks to revitalize itself through tourism and culture.

Its history dates back to the 16th century, and some original parts, such as the ribbed vaults and the doorway at the foot, date back to the time of the Catholic Monarchs. Over the years, extensions and modifications were made, including the replacement of the roof in the 19th century. During the Civil War, the church suffered damage to its artistic heritage. I hope you enjoy exploring this beautiful place of worship! 🙏⛪
It is located at the foot of the hill with the same name. It is a charming town with a beautiful old town.
